# HexParse Highlight -- LSP Architecture

> Generated by GLM-5V-Turbo

## Overview

The Language Server follows a **plugin registry** architecture. Each mod integration is defined as a self-contained `PluginDef` object that registers its own completion items, hover text, value extraction logic, and validation rules.

```
src/
  types.ts          -- PluginDef / ValueExtractor / TokenValidator interfaces
  plugins/
    index.ts        -- Registry: aggregates all plugins, exports flat accessors
    core.ts         -- Built-in types (num_, mask_, vec, entity_, etc.)
    *.ts            -- One file per external mod plugin (hexal, moreIotas, ...)
  hover.ts          -- Hover handler (table-driven dispatch)
  validation.ts     -- Validation handler (table-driven dispatch)
  completion.ts     -- Completion handler (prefix matching)
```

---

## PluginDef Interface

Defined in [`types.ts`](types.ts). Every plugin module exports one `PluginDef`:

```typescript
interface PluginDef {
    name: string                              // Human-readable name for debugging

    // -- Completion --
    prefixes: PrefixEntry[]                    // Prefix-based completion entries

    // -- Hover --
    hovers: HoverEntry                        // prefix -> i18n key map
    valueExtractors?: Record<string, ValueExtractor>  // Special formatting fns
    emptyDefaults?: Record<string, string>    // Fallback when suffix is empty

    // -- Validation --
    validators?: TokenValidator[]              // Per-token validation rules
}
```

### 1. Completion (`prefixes`)

Registers prefix-triggered completion items.

```typescript
export const myPlugin: PluginDef = {
    name: 'myMod',
    prefixes: [
        {
            prefix: 'thing_',                 // Trigger when user types "thing_"
            entries: [
                {
                    label: 'thing_',           // Completion label
                    kind: CompletionItemKind.Class,
                    detail: 'plugin.thing.detail',       // i18n key
                    documentation: 'plugin.thing.doc',   // i18n key
                    insertText: 'thing_${1:value}',    // Snippet template (optional)
                },
            ],
        },
    ],
}
```

**Fields on each Entry:**

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `label` | `string` | Yes | Display label in completion list |
| `kind` | `CompletionItemKind` | Yes | Icon type (see VSCode kinds) |
| `detail` | `string` (i18n key) | No | Detail line below label |
| `documentation` | `string` (i18n key) | No | Documentation panel content |
| `insertText` | `string` | No | Snippet template with `${n:placeholder}` |

### 2. Hover (`hovers` + `valueExtractors` + `emptyDefaults`)

#### Basic hover registration

```typescript
hovers: {
    thing_: 'hover.thing',   // i18n key; use {value} placeholder for extracted value
}
```

The framework extracts the suffix after the prefix and passes it as `{value}` to the i18n template.
For example, typing `thing_abc123` shows `{value}` = `"abc123"`.

#### Custom value formatting (`valueExtractors`)

When the raw suffix needs transformation before display (e.g. parsing vector components), register a `ValueExtractor`:

```typescript
import { PluginDef, ValueExtractor } from '../types'

const extractThing: ValueExtractor = (suffix) => {
    const parts = suffix.split('_')
    return `[${parts[0] ?? '?'}, ${parts[1] ?? '?'}]`
}

// Key must match a key in `hovers`
export const myPlugin: PluginDef = {
    hovers: { thing_: 'hover.thing' },
    valueExtractors: { thing_: extractThing },
}
```

#### Empty suffix defaults (`emptyDefaults`)

When a user types only the prefix without a value (e.g. just `num_`), specify what to show:

```typescript
emptyDefaults: { num_: '0' }   // num_ with no number -> Value: 0
```

Prefixes not listed here get an empty string `""` as default.

### 3. Validation (`validators`)

Register per-token validation rules. Each validator has two phases:

```typescript
validators: [
    {
        // Phase 1: Quick filter -- does this validator apply?
        test: (tokenText: string) => boolean,

        // Phase 2: Full validation -- return error info or null if valid
        validate: (tokenText: string) => { key: string; params: Record } | null,

        // Optional severity (default: Error)
        severity?: DiagnosticSeverity,
    },
]
```

**Example -- data overflow check:**

```typescript
{
    test: (t) => t.startsWith('matrix_') || t.startsWith('mat_'),
    validate: (t_text) => {
        const parts = t_text.slice(prefixLen).split('_').filter(Boolean)
        const rows = parseInt(parts[0], 10), cols = parseInt(parts[1], 10)
        const valCount = parts.length - 2
        if (!isNaN(rows) && !isNaN(cols) && rows > 0 && cols > 0 && valCount > rows * cols) {
            return { key: 'validation.matrixOverflow', params: { actual: valCount, expected: rows * cols, rows, cols } }
        }
        return null   // Valid
    },
    severity: DiagnosticSeverity.Error,
}
```

---

## Adding a New Plugin

1. Create `src/plugins/myMod.ts`, export a `PluginDef`
2. Add it to the `ALL_PLUGINS` array in [`plugins/index.ts`](plugins/index.ts)
3. Add i18n strings to both `locales/en/myMod.json` and `locales/zh-cn/myMod.json`

The hover, validation, and completion handlers pick up the new plugin automatically through table-driven dispatch.

---

## Data Flow

```
User types "num_42" in editor
        |
        v
  tokenizer.ts  -->  Token { text: "num_42" }
        |
        v
  hover.ts
    |-- HOVER_MAP lookup: "num_" matches corePlugin.hovers.num_ --> "hover.num"
    |-- allEmptyDefaults lookup: not needed (suffix non-empty)
    |-- allValueExtractors lookup: no extractor for "num_" --> raw suffix "42"
    |
    v
  i18n.ts  -->  tr("hover.num", { value: "42" })
        |
        v
  locales/en/core.json  -->  "**Number Pattern** ... Value: `42`"
        |
        v
  VSCode renders markdown hover tooltip
```
